metadata.title: Spanish Weather
metadata.description: Common weather expressions and phrases in Spanish.

sentences.original: Hoy hace sol en la playa.
sentences.translation: Today it is sunny at the beach.
I like it when it is sunny.
In winter it is very cold.

sentences.original: Cierra la ventana porque hace frío.
sentences.translation: Close the window because it is cold.
In summer it is always hot.
I am thirsty because it is hot.
Today it is raining a lot.
When it rains, I use an umbrella.
It snows a lot in the mountains.
Look out the window, it is snowing!
The sky is cloudy today.

sentences.original: Está nublado y parece que va a llover.
sentences.translation: It is cloudy and it looks like it is going to rain.

sentences.original: Hace mucho viento en la costa.
sentences.translation: It is very windy on the coast.

sentences.original: No podemos jugar tenis porque hace viento.
sentences.translation: We cannot play tennis because it is windy.
How is the weather in Madrid?
Tell me how the weather is outside.

sentences.original: Vamos al parque porque hace buen tiempo.
sentences.translation: Let's go to the park because the weather is good.
The weather is good for walking.

sentences.original: No quiero salir porque hace mal tiempo.
sentences.translation: I don't want to go out because the weather is bad.

sentences.original: Hace mal tiempo este fin de semana.
sentences.translation: The weather is bad this weekend.
